tips.chestCircumference: Measure chest circumference at the fullest part
tips.bodyLength: Body length is measured from shoulder to desired hem
tips.easeNext: Add ease for comfort - this will be handled in the next section
tips.headCircumference: Measure head circumference around the forehead
tips.hatHeight: Hat height includes any turn-back brim

fields.chest_circumference_description: Measure around the fullest part of the chest/bust

fields.shoulder_width_description: Measure from shoulder point to shoulder point
fields.torso_length_description: Measure from nape of neck to waist

fields.neck_circumference_description: Measure around the base of the neck

guide.tip_2: Keep the tape snug but not tight

gauge.title: Gauge Profiles
gauge.subtitle: Manage your knitting and crochet gauge measurements
gauge.no_profiles_description: You haven't created any gauge profiles yet. Create your first profile to get started.
gauge.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

form.description_create: Enter your gauge measurements to create a new profile
form.profile_name_placeholder: e.g., Worsted Weight Wool, DK Cotton
form.notes_placeholder: Add any additional notes about this gauge...

info.gauge_explanation: Gauge is the number of stitches and rows per inch or centimeter in your knitting or crochet work. It's crucial for ensuring your finished project matches the intended size.
measure_steps: Create a gauge swatch larger than 10cm x 10cm using your yarn and needle/hook
Work in the stitch pattern specified in your pattern
Block your swatch as you would the finished project
Measure and count stitches and rows over exactly 10cm
Record your measurements for future reference

ease.title: Ease Preferences
ease.subtitle: Manage your garment fit preferences and ease values
ease.no_preferences: No ease preferences found. Create your first one!
ease.create_description: Define your preferred ease values for different garment areas

form.preference_name_placeholder: e.g., Classic Sweater Fit, Tight Tank Top
form.categories_description: Choose a predefined category to quickly set up your ease values, then customize as needed.
form.notes_placeholder: Optional notes about this ease preference...

validation.preference_name_too_long: Preference name must be 255 characters or less
validation.measurement_unit_required: Measurement unit is required for absolute ease
validation.bust_ease_number: Bust ease must be a valid number
validation.waist_ease_number: Waist ease must be a valid number
validation.hip_ease_number: Hip ease must be a valid number
validation.sleeve_ease_number: Sleeve ease must be a valid number
validation.bust_ease_range: Bust ease must be between -50 and 100
validation.waist_ease_range: Waist ease must be between -50 and 100
validation.hip_ease_range: Hip ease must be between -50 and 100
validation.sleeve_ease_range: Sleeve ease must be between -50 and 100
validation.notes_too_long: Notes must be 1000 characters or less

messages.name_exists: An ease preference with this name already exists
messages.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this ease preference?

info.ease_explanation: Ease is the difference between your body measurements and the finished garment measurements. It determines how the garment will fit.
info.negative_ease_desc: Negative ease: Garment is smaller than body measurements (tight fit)
info.zero_ease_desc: Zero ease: Garment matches body measurements exactly
info.positive_ease_desc: Positive ease: Garment is larger than body measurements (loose fit)

validation.brand_name_too_long: Brand name must be 255 characters or less

validation.skein_yardage_positive: Skein yardage must be a positive number

validation.fiber_percentage_range: Fiber percentage must be between 0 and 100

stitchPattern.title: Stitch Pattern
stitchPattern.subtitle: Select and manage stitch patterns for your projects
stitchPattern.gaugeReminder: Remember to measure your gauge in the selected stitch pattern
stitchPattern.gaugeNotice: Your gauge (number of stitches and rows per inch/cm) can vary significantly between different stitch patterns. Always create a gauge swatch in your chosen stitch pattern before starting your project.
stitchPattern.step1: Create a gauge swatch in this stitch pattern
stitchPattern.step3: Use this information for pattern calculations
stitchPattern.noSearchResults: No stitch patterns found matching your search
stitchPattern.notFoundDescription: The requested stitch pattern could not be found.
stitchPattern.gaugeNote: Always create a gauge swatch before starting your project
stitchPattern.tensionNote: Your tension may affect the appearance of the pattern
stitchPattern.yarnNote: Different yarn weights will produce different results
stitchPattern.practiceNote: Practice the pattern on a small swatch first if you're unfamiliar with it

about_ease.title: About Ease
about_ease.description: is the difference between your body measurements and the finished garment measurements. It determines how the garment will fit and feel when worn.
about_ease.negative_ease_desc: Garment is smaller than body measurements (stretchy fabrics)
about_ease.positive_ease_desc: Garment is larger than body measurements

fit_guide.title: Fit Guide
fit_guide.very_close_fitting_desc: Negative ease, body-hugging fit. Best with stretchy yarns.
fit_guide.close_fitting_desc: Zero to minimal ease, fitted but comfortable.
fit_guide.classic_desc: Slight positive ease, traditional comfortable fit.
fit_guide.relaxed_desc: Moderate positive ease, loose and comfortable.
fit_guide.oversized_desc: Significant positive ease, very loose and flowing.

yarn_weight.title: Yarn Weight Impact
yarn_weight.description: Different yarn weights affect how ease translates to the final garment:
yarn_weight.fingering_sport_desc: More precise ease control, good for fitted garments
yarn_weight.dk_worsted_desc: Balanced drape and structure, versatile for most fits
yarn_weight.bulky_desc: Adds volume, may require less ease for desired fit

measurements.description: Manage your body measurements for accurate sizing

ease_preferences.title: Ease Preferences
ease_preferences.description: Save and manage your personal ease preferences

gauge_comparison.description: Compare pattern gauge with your gauge and get adjustments needed

morphology_advisor.tool_description: Get personalized advice for adapting patterns to your specific body characteristics
morphology_advisor.no_results_message: No specific advice found for the selected characteristics. Try selecting other characteristics.
morphology_advisor.select_characteristics_description: Choose the characteristics that best describe your body shape to get personalized fitting advice
morphology_advisor.title: Body Morphology Advisor
morphology_advisor.description: Get advice on adapting patterns to your body shape
morphology_advisor.page_subtitle: Get personalized advice for adapting patterns to your specific body characteristics

about.title: About This Tool
about.description: This tool helps you understand how to modify knitting and crochet patterns to better fit your unique body characteristics. Get specific advice on pattern adjustments, ease considerations, and fitting techniques.

morphological_fitting.title: About Morphological Fitting
morphological_fitting.description: The practice of adjusting patterns to accommodate individual body characteristics that differ from standard sizing.
morphological_fitting.full_bust_desc: Requires bust shaping adjustments like dart placement or short rows
morphological_fitting.broad_shoulders_desc: May need shoulder width adjustments and sleeve cap modifications
morphological_fitting.long_short_torso_desc: Requires length adjustments to bodice and waist placement
morphological_fitting.particular_posture_desc: Such as sway back, requiring back length and fit adjustments

fitting_tips.title: General Fitting Tips
fitting_tips.accurate_measurements_desc: Use a flexible tape measure and have someone help for hard-to-reach areas
fitting_tips.make_swatch_desc: Always knit/crochet a swatch with your chosen yarn and needles/hook
fitting_tips.adjust_gradually_desc: Make small adjustments and test fit frequently during construction
fitting_tips.document_changes_desc: Keep notes of successful modifications for future projects

common_adjustments.title: Common Adjustments
common_adjustments.bust_adjustment_desc: Add short rows or darts for better bust fit
common_adjustments.shoulder_adjustment_desc: Modify shoulder width and slope for proper fit
common_adjustments.length_adjustment_desc: Adjust torso and sleeve lengths to your proportions
common_adjustments.waist_adjustment_desc: Shape waist placement and curves to your body

related_tools.title: Related Tools
related_tools.measurements_management_desc: Store and manage your body measurements
related_tools.ease_advisor_desc: Get recommendations for appropriate ease amounts

characteristics.full_bust_description: Larger bust measurement requiring additional shaping
characteristics.sway_back_description: Lower back curves inward more than average
characteristics.broad_shoulders_description: Shoulder width wider than average proportionally
characteristics.sloping_shoulders_description: Shoulders that slope downward more than average
characteristics.long_torso_description: Torso longer than average in proportion to height
characteristics.short_torso_description: Torso shorter than average in proportion to height
characteristics.long_arms_description: Arms longer than average requiring longer sleeves
characteristics.short_arms_description: Arms shorter than average requiring shorter sleeves

morphology_advisor.related_measurements_link: Take precise body measurements for best results
morphology_advisor.related_ease_link: Use the Ease Advisor tool for garment-specific recommendations
morphology_advisor.pattern_selection_tips: Consider these characteristics when selecting patterns

yarn_quantity_estimator.description: Estimate the amount of yarn needed for your project based on gauge, yarn specifications, and project type
yarn_quantity_estimator.subtitle: Get a rough estimate of yarn requirements for planning your project

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This is a rough estimate and actual yarn usage may vary based on:
disclaimer.recommendation: Always purchase a little extra yarn to account for variations and potential mistakes.

step_instructions.template_selection: Step 1: Select a pattern structure template below that best matches the piece you want to resize.
step_instructions.original_gauge: Step 2: Enter the gauge (tension) information from the ORIGINAL pattern.
step_instructions.original_pattern: Step 3: Fill in the key measurements from the ORIGINAL pattern (e.g., cast-on stitches, finished width).
step_instructions.new_gauge: Step 2: Enter the gauge (tension) information for YOUR yarn.
step_instructions.new_dimensions: Step 4: Specify the NEW dimensions you want for your piece.
step_instructions.calculate: Step 5: Click 'Calculate' to get new stitch and row instructions.

messages.calculation_error: Failed to calculate new pattern values

disclaimers.title: Important Disclaimers
disclaimers.content: This tool provides numerical rescaling only and cannot account for:
limitations: Complex pattern features beyond basic measurements
disclaimers.recommendation: Use your knitting/crochet judgment to verify results and make necessary adjustments.
disclaimers.note: This is a starting point for pattern adaptation, not a complete solution.

help.title: How to Use This Tool
steps: Select the pattern structure that matches your existing pattern
Enter the original pattern's gauge and key measurements
Enter your own gauge (from swatching or saved profiles)
tips: Always swatch with your yarn to get accurate gauge
Consider how yarn weight differences might affect drape
Test calculations on a small sample first if unsure

text_parser.title: Import from Text
text_parser.description: Paste your pattern text using the structured format below. The parser will automatically extract gauge and measurement values.
text_parser.placeholder:
Paste your structured pattern text here...

Example:
PATTERN_NAME: My Pattern
ORIGINAL_GAUGE_STITCHES_PER_10CM: 20
ORIGINAL_GAUGE_ROWS_PER_10CM: 28
...

format_help.title: Text Format Guide
tips: Use keywords exactly as shown (case insensitive)
One value per line in KEYWORD: value format
Unit suffixes (_CM or _IN) help automatic detection

userRole.title: User Information
userRole.notSignedIn: You are not signed in. Your role is

triangular_shawl.title: Triangular Shawl
triangular_shawl.description: Calculator for triangular shawls with different construction methods

top_down_center_out.name: Top-Down Center-Out
top_down_center_out.description: Starts with a few stitches at the center top, with increases on both sides and around a central spine to form a downward-pointing triangle

side_to_side.name: Side-to-Side
side_to_side.description: Starts with a few stitches at one point, increases on one side until maximum depth, then decreases on the same side to form the other half

bottom_up.name: Bottom-Up
bottom_up.description: Starts with a large number of stitches for the longest side of the triangle and decreases regularly on both sides to form the top point

dimensions.wingspan_description: Total width of the shawl from end to end
dimensions.depth_description: Depth from center to triangle point

instructions.place_markers: Place markers for center spine if desired

warnings.dimension_difference: Calculated dimensions differ from target dimensions
warnings.wingspan_variance: Actual wingspan ({{actual}}cm) differs from target ({{target}}cm) by more than {{percentage}}%
warnings.depth_variance: Actual depth ({{actual}}cm) differs from target ({{target}}cm) by more than {{percentage}}%

undoRedoDemo.description: This demo showcases the undo/redo functionality for pattern definition changes. Every field modification creates an undo point.

instructions.title: How to Test Undo/Redo
instructions.step1: Create a new pattern and select a garment type
instructions.step2: Navigate to any section and fill in the demo fields
instructions.step3: Every field change creates an undo point
instructions.step4: Use the undo/redo buttons in the top-right corner
instructions.step5: Watch the history counter and button states change
instructions.step6: Configure history size and enable/disable functionality
